entries.title: Luke Founds the Yavin IV Jedi Praxeum

content.fact: Luke establishes a Jedi academy on Yavin IV to begin rebuilding the Jedi Order in institutional form.
content.injection: After the Jedi Academy founding, Luke can have students, training obligations, and institutional failures; before it, he remains a mostly solitary Jedi teacher.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Luke Founds the Jedi Academy.
Respect story-position bounds from Luke Founds the Jedi Academy to Luke Founds the Jedi Academy.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Praxeum Is Not the Old Jedi Order

context.label: Jedi Academy founding era
context.phase: Jedi Academy Era to Mid New Republic

content.fact: Luke’s academy lacks the Old Republic Jedi Order’s full archives, hierarchy, and institutional memory.
content.injection: Write early New Jedi training as improvised, rediscovered, and vulnerable to mistakes rather than a perfect restoration of the old Order.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Jedi Academy founding era.
Respect story-position bounds from Luke Founds the Jedi Academy to Jedi Praxeum Stabilizes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Early Jedi Students Have Mixed Backgrounds

context.label: Jedi Academy founding era
context.phase: Jedi Academy Era to Mid New Republic

content.fact: Luke’s early students are often adults or young adults with varied histories, trauma, politics, and uneven Force awareness.
content.injection: Do not write the first Praxeum as a clean child-only temple; students arrive with prior lives and unstable assumptions.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Jedi Academy founding era.
Respect story-position bounds from Luke Founds the Jedi Academy to Jedi Praxeum Stabilizes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Exar Kun’s Spirit Is a Hidden Academy Threat

context.label: Exar Kun Defeated at Yavin IV

content.fact: Exar Kun’s dark-side spirit on Yavin IV threatens Luke’s students before they fully understand the danger.
content.injection: During early Praxeum context, let Yavin IV carry hidden dark-side risk; do not have all students immediately understand Exar Kun’s presence.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Exar Kun Defeated at Yavin IV.
Respect story-position bounds from Exar Kun Defeated at Yavin IV to Exar Kun Defeated at Yavin IV.

entries.title: Kyp Durron Combines Trauma and Dangerous Power

context.label: Jedi Academy founding era
context.phase: Jedi Academy Era to Mid New Republic

content.fact: Kyp Durron is powerful, traumatized, and vulnerable to dark-side manipulation, especially around Exar Kun and the Sun Crusher.
content.injection: In Jedi Academy scenes, write Kyp as more than reckless; his trauma, power, guilt, and manipulation should all matter.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Jedi Academy founding era.
Respect story-position bounds from Luke Founds the Jedi Academy to Jedi Praxeum Stabilizes.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

entries.title: Sun Crusher Is an Extreme Superweapon

context.label: Kyp Durron and Sun Crusher Crisis

content.fact: The Sun Crusher is an extreme superweapon capable of destroying stars, making it far beyond ordinary military hardware.
content.injection: If the Sun Crusher appears, treat it as a strategic and moral crisis, not a normal starfighter or bargaining chip.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Kyp Durron and Sun Crusher Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Kyp Durron and Sun Crusher Crisis to Kyp Durron and Sun Crusher Crisis.

entries.title: Maw Installation Is a Hidden Imperial Weapons Lab

context.label: Kyp Durron and Sun Crusher Crisis

content.fact: The Maw Installation is a hidden Imperial research facility tied to superweapon development.
content.injection: Before discovery, do not let ordinary New Republic characters know the Maw’s full purpose; when active, use it as secret weapons-infrastructure lore.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Kyp Durron and Sun Crusher Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Kyp Durron and Sun Crusher Crisis to Kyp Durron and Sun Crusher Crisis.

entries.title: Daala Represents Isolated Imperial Holdout Power

content.fact: Admiral Daala emerges from isolated Imperial weapons infrastructure rather than normal Remnant politics.
content.injection: When Daala appears, distinguish her from Pellaeon-style professional Remnant politics and Thrawn-style strategic command.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Daala and Imperial Holdout Campaigns.
Respect story-position bounds from Daala and Imperial Holdout Campaigns to Daala and Imperial Holdout Campaigns.

entries.title: Corran’s Jedi Training Complicates Pilot Identity

context.label: Jedi Academy founding era
context.phase: Jedi Academy Era to Mid New Republic

content.fact: Corran Horn’s Jedi path complicates his existing identity as investigator, pilot, husband, and Rogue Squadron veteran.
content.injection: When Corran trains as a Jedi, keep his CorSec/Rogue habits and skepticism active rather than making him instantly temple-shaped.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Jedi Academy founding era.
Respect story-position bounds from Luke Founds the Jedi Academy to Jedi Praxeum Stabilizes.

entries.title: New Jedi Order Means Luke’s Rebuilt Order Here

context.label: Jedi Academy founding era
context.phase: Jedi Academy Era to Mid New Republic

content.fact: In pre-NJO Legends context, “New Jedi Order” can mean Luke’s rebuilding Jedi movement, not necessarily the later Yuuzhan Vong-era book series.
content.injection: In Jedi Academy founding era, disambiguate “New Jedi Order” by context: before 25 ABY, it usually refers to Luke’s restored Jedi institution, not the NJO invasion era.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Jedi Academy founding era.
Respect story-position bounds from Luke Founds the Jedi Academy to Jedi Praxeum Stabilizes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
